steps.instruction: Bring hands high above head, knuckles touching.
steps.helpDetail: The locking mechanism of the zip tie should be between your hands or facing outward. Do not have the lock against your skin.

steps.instruction: Explosively bring hands down into your stomach.
steps.helpDetail: Simultaneously flare your elbows out like chicken wings. The force breaks the locking prow.
Remove shoelace, thread through tie. Loop ends on feet (bicycle motion). Saw back and forth to melt plastic.

techniques.name: Door Barricade
techniques.subtitle: Secure a room without a lock

steps.instruction: Use a door wedge / shim under the door.
steps.helpDetail: Kick a rubber wedge or wooden shim hard under the door. This prevents opening from the outside.

steps.instruction: Tie the door handle to a heavy immovable object.
steps.helpDetail: Use belt or cordage to tie handle to a bed frame or radiator. Reduce slack to zero.

steps.instruction: Chair method: Wedge chair back under the door knob.
steps.helpDetail: Tilt chair so top of backrest is under the knob, legs are on floor. Pressure from opening drives chair into floor.

techniques.name: Gray Man Protocol
techniques.subtitle: Blend into crowds and avoid attention

steps.instruction: Remove all tactical gear and identifying patches.
steps.helpDetail: No camo, no morale patches, no MOLLE packs. Look like a refugee or local commuter.

steps.instruction: Adjust walking pace to match the crowd baseline.
steps.helpDetail: Do not run (attracts pursuit). Do not loiter (attracts security). Flow with the 'river' of people.

steps.instruction: Avoid eye contact but maintain situational awareness.
steps.helpDetail: Scan using reflections (shop windows, phone screen). Direct eye contact is a threat signal.

steps.instruction: Choose the side window furthest from occupants/gear.
steps.helpDetail: Windshields are laminated (won't shatter). Side windows are tempered (will shatter).

steps.instruction: Strike the bottom corner of the glass, not the center.
steps.helpDetail: The center flexes. The corners are rigid break points. Use a spark plug ceramic, center punch, or sharp rock.

steps.instruction: Look away and cover eyes precisely when striking.
steps.helpDetail: Glass dust will explode inward. Clear remaining jagged edges with a tool before reaching in.

steps.instruction: Walk on hard surfaces (rock, pavement) to reduce footprints.
steps.helpDetail: Avoid mud, soft sand, and snow. Step on rocks or logs (disturbed environment detection).

steps.instruction: Fish-hook maneuver: Walk past your hide site, then loop back.
steps.helpDetail: Walk 100m past where you intend to hide, then circle back in a wide loop to watch your own trail.

steps.instruction: Avoid breaking branches or disturbing spider webs.
steps.helpDetail: These are 'ground sign' indicators. Move slowly and deliberately.
